# Configuration

Moltis is configured through `moltis.toml`, located in `~/.config/moltis/` by default.

On first run, a complete configuration file is generated with sensible defaults. You can edit it to customize behavior.

## Configuration File Location

| Platform | Default Path |
|----------|--------------|
| macOS/Linux | `~/.config/moltis/moltis.toml` |
| Custom | Set via `--config-dir` or `MOLTIS_CONFIG_DIR` |

## Basic Settings

```toml
[server]
port = 13131                    # HTTP/WebSocket port
bind = "0.0.0.0"               # Listen address

[identity]
name = "Moltis"                 # Agent display name

[tools]
agent_timeout_secs = 600        # Agent run timeout (seconds, 0 = no timeout)
agent_max_iterations = 25       # Max tool call iterations per run
```

## LLM Providers

Configure providers through the web UI or directly in `moltis.toml`. API keys can be set
via environment variables (e.g. `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`) or
in the config file.

```toml
[providers]
offered = ["anthropic", "openai", "gemini"]

[providers.anthropic]
enabled = true

[providers.openai]
enabled = true
models = ["gpt-5.3", "gpt-5.2"]
stream_transport = "sse"        # "sse", "websocket", or "auto"

[providers.gemini]
enabled = true
models = ["gemini-2.5-flash-preview-05-20", "gemini-2.0-flash"]

[providers.local-llm]
enabled = true
models = ["qwen2.5-coder-7b-q4_k_m"]

[chat]
priority_models = ["gpt-5.2"]
```

See [Providers](providers.md) for the full list of supported providers and configuration options.

## Sandbox Configuration

Commands run inside isolated containers for security:

```toml
[tools.exec.sandbox]
mode = "all"                    # "off", "non-main", or "all"
scope = "session"               # "command", "session", or "global"
workspace_mount = "ro"          # "ro", "rw", or "none"
home_persistence = "shared"     # "off", "session", or "shared" (default: "shared")
# shared_home_dir = "/path/to/shared-home"  # Optional path for shared mode
backend = "auto"                # "auto", "docker", or "apple-container"
no_network = true

# Packages installed in the sandbox image
packages = [
    "curl",
    "git",
    "jq",
    "tmux",
    "python3",
    "python3-pip",
    "nodejs",
    "npm",
    "golang-go",
]
```

```admonish info
When you modify the packages list and restart, Moltis automatically rebuilds the sandbox image with a new tag.
```

## Web Search

Configure the built-in `web_search` tool:

```toml
[tools.web.search]
enabled = true
provider = "brave"               # "brave" or "perplexity"
max_results = 5                  # 1-10
timeout_seconds = 30
cache_ttl_minutes = 15
duckduckgo_fallback = false      # Default: do not use DuckDuckGo fallback
# api_key = "..."                # Brave key, or use BRAVE_API_KEY

[tools.web.search.perplexity]
# api_key = "..."                # Or use PERPLEXITY_API_KEY / OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# base_url = "..."               # Optional override
# model = "perplexity/sonar-pro" # Optional override
```

If no search API key is configured:

- with `duckduckgo_fallback = false` (default), Moltis returns a clear hint to set `BRAVE_API_KEY` or `PERPLEXITY_API_KEY`
- with `duckduckgo_fallback = true`, Moltis attempts DuckDuckGo HTML search, which may hit CAPTCHA/rate limits

## Chat Message Queue

When a new message arrives while an agent run is already active, Moltis can either
replay queued messages one-by-one or merge them into a single follow-up message.

```toml
[chat]
message_queue_mode = "followup"  # Default: one-by-one replay

# Options:
#   "followup" - Queue each message and run them sequentially
#   "collect"  - Merge queued text and run once after the active run
```

## Memory System

Long-term memory uses embeddings for semantic search:

```toml
[memory]
backend = "builtin"             # Or "qmd"
provider = "openai"             # Or "local", "ollama", "custom"
model = "text-embedding-3-small"
citations = "auto"              # "on", "off", or "auto"
llm_reranking = false
session_export = false
```

## Authentication

Authentication is **only required when accessing Moltis from a non-localhost address**. When running on `localhost` or `127.0.0.1`, no authentication is needed by default.

When you access Moltis from a network address (e.g., `http://192.168.1.100:13131`), a one-time setup code is printed to the terminal. Use it to set up a password or passkey.

```toml
[auth]
disabled = false                # Set true to disable auth entirely
```

```admonish warning
Only set `disabled = true` if Moltis is running on a trusted private network. Never expose an unauthenticated instance to the internet.
```

## Hooks

Configure lifecycle hooks:

```toml
[hooks]
[[hooks.hooks]]
name = "my-hook"
command = "./hooks/my-hook.sh"
events = ["BeforeToolCall", "AfterToolCall"]
timeout = 5                     # Timeout in seconds

[hooks.hooks.env]
MY_VAR = "value"               # Environment variables for the hook
```

See [Hooks](hooks.md) for the full hook system documentation.

## MCP Servers

Connect to Model Context Protocol servers:

```toml
[mcp]

[mcp.servers.filesystem]
command = "npx"
args = ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/path/to/allowed"]

[mcp.servers.github]
command = "npx"
args = ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
env = { GITHUB_TOKEN = "ghp_..." }
```

## Telegram Integration

```toml
[channels.telegram.my-bot]
token = "123456:ABC..."
allowed_users = [123456789]     # Telegram user IDs allowed to chat
```

## Discord Integration

```toml
[channels]
offered = ["telegram", "discord"]

[channels.discord.my-bot]
token = "MTIzNDU2Nzg5.example.bot-token"
dm_policy = "allowlist"
mention_mode = "mention"
allowlist = ["your_username"]
```

See [Discord](discord.md) for full configuration reference and setup instructions.

## TLS / HTTPS

```toml
[tls]
enabled = true
cert_path = "~/.config/moltis/cert.pem"
key_path = "~/.config/moltis/key.pem"
# If paths don't exist, a self-signed certificate is generated

# Port for the plain-HTTP redirect / CA-download server.
# Defaults to the server port + 1 when not set.
# http_redirect_port = 13132
```

Override via environment variable: `MOLTIS_TLS__HTTP_REDIRECT_PORT=8080`.

## Tailscale Integration

Expose Moltis over your Tailscale network:

```toml
[tailscale]
mode = "serve"                  # "off", "serve", or "funnel"
reset_on_exit = true
```

## Observability

```toml
[metrics]
enabled = true
prometheus_endpoint = true
```

## Process Environment Variables (`[env]`)

The `[env]` section injects variables into the Moltis process at startup.
This is useful in Docker deployments where passing individual `-e` flags is
inconvenient, or when you want API keys stored in the config file rather
than the host environment.

```toml
[env]
BRAVE_API_KEY = "your-brave-key"
OPENROUTER_API_KEY = "sk-or-..."
ELEVENLABS_API_KEY = "..."
```

**Precedence**: existing process environment variables are never overwritten.
If `BRAVE_API_KEY` is already set via `docker -e` or the host shell, the
`[env]` value is skipped. This means `docker -e` always wins.

```admonish info title="Settings UI vs [env]"
Environment variables configured through the Settings UI (Settings >
Environment) are also injected into the Moltis process at startup.
Precedence: host/`docker -e` > config `[env]` > Settings UI.
```

## Environment Variables

All settings can be overridden via environment variables:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `MOLTIS_CONFIG_DIR` | Configuration directory |
| `MOLTIS_DATA_DIR` | Data directory |
| `MOLTIS_SERVER__PORT` | Server port override |
| `MOLTIS_SERVER__BIND` | Server bind address override |
| `MOLTIS_TOOLS__AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECS` | Agent run timeout override |
| `MOLTIS_TOOLS__AGENT_MAX_ITERATIONS` | Agent loop iteration cap override |

## CLI Flags

```bash
moltis --config-dir /path/to/config --data-dir /path/to/data
```

## Complete Example

```toml
[server]
port = 13131
bind = "0.0.0.0"

[identity]
name = "Atlas"

[tools]
agent_timeout_secs = 600
agent_max_iterations = 25

[providers]
offered = ["anthropic", "openai", "gemini"]

[tools.exec.sandbox]
mode = "all"
scope = "session"
workspace_mount = "ro"
home_persistence = "session"
# shared_home_dir = "/path/to/shared-home"
backend = "auto"
no_network = true
packages = ["curl", "git", "jq", "python3", "nodejs", "golang-go"]

[memory]
backend = "builtin"
provider = "openai"
model = "text-embedding-3-small"

[auth]
disabled = false

[hooks]
[[hooks.hooks]]
name = "audit-log"
command = "./hooks/audit.sh"
events = ["BeforeToolCall"]
timeout = 5
```

content.content:
1→# Discord
     2→
     3→Moltis can connect to Discord as a bot, letting you chat with your agent from
     4→any Discord server or DM. The integration uses Discord's
     5→[Gateway API](https://discord.com/developers/docs/events/gateway) via a
     6→persistent WebSocket connection — no public URL or webhook endpoint is required.
     7→
     8→## How It Works
     9→
    10→```
    11→┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    12→│                   Discord Gateway                     │
    13→│              (wss://gateway.discord.gg)               │
    14→└──────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
    15→                   │  persistent WebSocket
    16→                   ▼
    17→┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    18→│               moltis-discord crate                    │
    19→│  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  │
    20→│  │  Handler   │  │  Outbound  │  │     Plugin     │  │
    21→│  │ (inbound)  │  │ (replies)  │  │  (lifecycle)   │  │
    22→│  └────────────┘  └────────────┘  └────────────────┘  │
    23→└──────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
    24→                   │
    25→                   ▼
    26→┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    27→│                 Moltis Gateway                        │
    28→│         (chat dispatch, tools, memory)                │
    29→└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    30→```
    31→
    32→The bot connects **outward** to Discord's servers. Unlike Microsoft Teams
    33→(which requires an inbound webhook), Discord needs no port forwarding, no
    34→public domain, and no TLS certificate. This makes it especially easy to run
    35→on a home machine or behind a NAT.
    36→
    37→## Prerequisites
    38→
    39→Before configuring Moltis, create a Discord bot:
    40→
    41→1. Go to the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications)
    42→2. Click **New Application** and give it a name
    43→3. Navigate to **Bot** in the left sidebar
    44→4. Click **Reset Token** and copy the bot token
    45→5. Under **Privileged Gateway Intents**, enable **Message Content Intent**
    46→6. Navigate to **OAuth2 → URL Generator**
    47→   - Scopes: `bot`
    48→   - Bot Permissions: `Send Messages`, `Attach Files`, `Read Message History`, `Add Reactions`
    49→7. Copy the generated URL and open it to invite the bot to your server
    50→
    51→```admonish warning
    52→The bot token is a secret — treat it like a password. Never commit it to
    53→version control. Moltis stores it with `secrecy::Secret` and redacts it from
    54→logs, but your `moltis.toml` file is plain text on disk. Consider using
    55→[Vault](vault.md) for encryption at rest.
    56→```
    57→
    58→## Configuration
    59→
    60→Add a `[channels.discord.<account-id>]` section to your `moltis.toml`:
    61→
    62→```toml
    63→[channels.discord.my-bot]
    64→token = "MTIzNDU2Nzg5.example.bot-token"
    65→```
    66→
    67→Make sure `"discord"` is included in `channels.offered` so the Web UI shows
    68→the Discord option:
    69→
    70→```toml
    71→[channels]
    72→offered = ["telegram", "discord"]
    73→```
    74→
    75→### Configuration Fields
    76→
    77→| Field | Required | Default | Description |
    78→|-------|----------|---------|-------------|
    79→| `token` | **yes** | — | Discord bot token from the Developer Portal |
    80→| `dm_policy` | no | `"allowlist"` | Who can DM the bot: `"open"`, `"allowlist"`, or `"disabled"` |
    81→| `group_policy` | no | `"open"` | Who can talk to the bot in guild channels: `"open"`, `"allowlist"`, or `"disabled"` |
    82→| `mention_mode` | no | `"mention"` | When the bot responds in guilds: `"always"`, `"mention"` (only when @mentioned), or `"none"` |
    83→| `allowlist` | no | `[]` | Discord usernames allowed to DM the bot (when `dm_policy = "allowlist"`) |
    84→| `guild_allowlist` | no | `[]` | Guild (server) IDs allowed to interact with the bot |
    85→| `model` | no | — | Override the default model for this channel |
    86→| `model_provider` | no | — | Provider for the overridden model |
    87→| `reply_to_message` | no | `false` | Send bot responses as Discord replies to the user's message |
    88→| `ack_reaction` | no | — | Emoji reaction added while processing (e.g. `"👀"`); omit to disable |
    89→| `activity` | no | — | Bot activity status text (e.g. `"with AI"`) |
    90→| `activity_type` | no | `"custom"` | Activity type: `"playing"`, `"listening"`, `"watching"`, `"competing"`, or `"custom"` |
    91→| `status` | no | `"online"` | Bot online status: `"online"`, `"idle"`, `"dnd"`, or `"invisible"` |
    92→| `otp_self_approval` | no | `true` | Enable OTP self-approval for non-allowlisted DM users |
    93→| `otp_cooldown_secs` | no | `300` | Cooldown in seconds after 3 failed OTP attempts |
    94→
    95→### Full Example
    96→
    97→```toml
    98→[channels]
    99→offered = ["telegram", "discord"]
   100→
   101→[channels.discord.my-bot]
   102→token = "MTIzNDU2Nzg5.example.bot-token"
   103→dm_policy = "allowlist"
   104→group_policy = "open"
   105→mention_mode = "mention"
   106→allowlist = ["alice", "bob"]
   107→guild_allowlist = ["123456789012345678"]
   108→reply_to_message = true
   109→ack_reaction = "👀"
   110→model = "gpt-4o"
   111→model_provider = "openai"
   112→activity = "with AI"
   113→activity_type = "custom"
   114→status = "online"
   115→otp_self_approval = true
   116→```
   117→
   118→## Access Control
   119→
   120→Discord uses the same gating system as Telegram and Microsoft Teams:
   121→
   122→### DM Policy
   123→
   124→Controls who can send direct messages to the bot.
   125→
   126→| Value | Behavior |
   127→|-------|----------|
   128→| `"allowlist"` | Only users listed in `allowlist` can DM (default) |
   129→| `"open"` | Anyone who can reach the bot can DM it |
   130→| `"disabled"` | DMs are silently ignored |
   131→
   132→### Group Policy
   133→
   134→Controls who can interact with the bot in guild (server) channels.
   135→
   136→| Value | Behavior |
   137→|-------|----------|
   138→| `"open"` | Bot responds in any guild channel (default) |
   139→| `"allowlist"` | Only guilds listed in `guild_allowlist` are allowed |
   140→| `"disabled"` | Guild messages are silently ignored |
   141→
   142→### Mention Mode
   143→
   144→Controls when the bot responds in guild channels (does not apply to DMs).
   145→
   146→| Value | Behavior |
   147→|-------|----------|
   148→| `"mention"` | Bot only responds when @mentioned (default) |
   149→| `"always"` | Bot responds to every message in allowed channels |
   150→| `"none"` | Bot never responds in guilds (useful for DM-only bots) |
   151→
   152→### Guild Allowlist
   153→
   154→If `guild_allowlist` is non-empty, messages from guilds **not** in the list are
   155→silently dropped — regardless of `group_policy`. This provides a server-level
   156→filter on top of the channel-level policy.
   157→
   158→### OTP Self-Approval
   159→
   160→When `dm_policy = "allowlist"` and `otp_self_approval = true` (the default),
   161→unknown users who DM the bot receive a verification challenge. The flow:
   162→
   163→1. User sends a DM to the bot
   164→2. Bot responds with a challenge prompt (the 6-digit code is **not** shown to the user)
   165→3. The code appears in the Moltis web UI under **Channels → Senders**
   166→4. The bot owner shares the code with the user out-of-band
   167→5. User replies with the 6-digit code
   168→6. On success, the user is automatically added to the allowlist
   169→
   170→After 3 failed attempts, the user is locked out for `otp_cooldown_secs` seconds
   171→(default: 300). Codes expire after 5 minutes.
   172→
   173→```admonish tip
   174→This is the same OTP mechanism used by the Telegram integration. It provides a
   175→simple access control flow without requiring manual allowlist management.
   176→```
   177→
   178→## Bot Presence
   179→
   180→Configure the bot's Discord presence (the "Playing..." / "Listening to..." status)
   181→using the `activity`, `activity_type`, and `status` fields:
   182→
   183→```toml
   184→[channels.discord.my-bot]
   185→token = "..."
   186→activity = "with AI"
   187→activity_type = "custom"  # or "playing", "listening", "watching", "competing"
   188→status = "online"         # or "idle", "dnd", "invisible"
   189→```
   190→
   191→The presence is set when the bot connects to the Discord gateway. If no activity
   192→or status is configured, the bot uses Discord's default (online, no activity).
   193→
   194→## Slash Commands
   195→
   196→The bot automatically registers native Discord slash commands when it connects:
   197→
   198→| Command | Description |
   199→|---------|-------------|
   200→| `/new` | Start a new chat session |
   201→| `/clear` | Clear the current session history |
   202→| `/compact` | Summarize the current session |
   203→| `/context` | Show session info (model, tokens, plugins) |
   204→| `/model` | List or switch the AI model |
   205→| `/sessions` | List or switch chat sessions |
   206→| `/agent` | List or switch agents |
   207→| `/help` | Show available commands |
   208→
   209→Slash commands appear in Discord's command palette (type `/` in any channel where
   210→the bot is present). Responses are ephemeral — only visible to the user who
   211→invoked the command.
   212→
   213→```admonish note
   214→Text-based `/` commands (e.g. typing `/model` as a regular message) continue to
   215→work alongside native slash commands. The native commands provide autocomplete and
   216→a better Discord-native experience.
   217→```
   218→
   219→## Web UI Setup
   220→
   221→You can also configure Discord through the web interface:
   222→
   223→1. Open **Settings → Channels**
   224→2. Click **Connect Discord**
   225→3. Enter an account ID (any alias) and your bot token
   226→4. Adjust DM policy, mention mode, and allowlist as needed
   227→5. Click **Connect**
   228→
   229→The same form is available during onboarding when Discord is in `channels.offered`.
   230→
   231→## Talking to Your Bot
   232→
   233→Once the bot is connected there are several ways to interact with it.
   234→
   235→### In a Server
   236→
   237→To use the bot in a Discord server you need to invite it first:
   238→
   239→1. Go to the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications)
   240→2. Select your application → **OAuth2 → URL Generator**
   241→3. Scopes: check **bot**
   242→4. Bot Permissions: check **Send Messages**, **Read Message History**, and **Add Reactions**
   243→5. Copy the generated URL and open it in your browser
   244→6. Select the server you want to add the bot to and confirm
   245→
   246→```admonish tip
   247→The Moltis web UI generates this invite link automatically when you paste your
   248→bot token. Look for the "Invite bot to a server" card in the Connect Discord
   249→dialog.
   250→```
   251→
   252→Once the bot is in your server, **@mention** it in any channel to get a
   253→response (assuming `mention_mode = "mention"`, the default). If you set
   254→`mention_mode = "always"` the bot responds to every message in allowed channels.
   255→
   256→### Via Direct Message
   257→
   258→You can DM the bot directly from Discord — no shared server required:
   259→
   260→1. Open Discord and go to **Direct Messages**
   261→2. Click the **New Message** icon (or **Find or start a conversation**)
   262→3. Search for the bot's username and select it
   263→4. Send a message
   264→
   265→```admonish note
   266→If `dm_policy` is set to `"allowlist"` (the default), make sure your Discord
   267→username is listed in the `allowlist` array — otherwise the bot will ignore your
   268→DMs. Set `dm_policy = "open"` to allow anyone to DM the bot.
   269→```
   270→
   271→### Without a Shared Server
   272→
   273→DMs work even if you and the bot don't share a server. Discord bots are
   274→reachable by username from any account. This makes DMs the simplest way to
   275→start chatting — just connect the bot in Moltis and message it directly.
   276→
   277→## Message Handling
   278→
   279→### Inbound Messages
   280→
   281→When a message arrives from Discord:
   282→
   283→1. Bot's own messages are ignored
   284→2. Guild allowlist is checked (if configured)
   285→3. DM/group policy is evaluated
   286→4. Mention mode is checked (guild messages only)
   287→5. Bot mention prefix (`@BotName`) is stripped from the message text
   288→6. The message is logged and dispatched to the chat engine
   289→7. Commands (messages starting with `/`) are dispatched to the command handler
   290→
   291→### Outbound Messages
   292→
   293→Discord enforces a **2,000-character limit** per message. Moltis automatically
   294→splits long responses into multiple messages, preferring to break at newline
   295→boundaries and avoiding splits inside fenced code blocks.
   296→
   297→Streaming uses **edit-in-place** — an initial message is sent after 30 characters
   298→and then updated every 500ms as tokens arrive. If the final text exceeds 2,000
   299→characters, the first message is edited to the limit and overflow is sent as
   300→follow-up messages.
   301→
   302→### Reply-to-Message
   303→
   304→Set `reply_to_message = true` to have the bot send responses as Discord replies
   305→(threaded to the user's original message). The first chunk of a multi-chunk or
   306→streamed response carries the reply reference; follow-up chunks are sent as
   307→regular messages.
   308→
   309→### Ack Reactions
   310→
   311→Set `ack_reaction = "👀"` (or any Unicode emoji) to have the bot react to the
   312→user's message when processing starts. The reaction is removed once the response
   313→is complete. This provides a visual indicator that the bot has seen the message
   314→and is working on a reply.
   315→
   316→## Crate Structure
   317→
   318→```
   319→crates/discord/
   320→├── Cargo.toml
   321→└── src/
   322→    ├── lib.rs         # Public exports
   323→    ├── commands.rs    # Native Discord slash command registration
   324→    ├── config.rs      # DiscordAccountConfig (token, policies, presence, OTP)
   325→    ├── error.rs       # Error enum (Config, Gateway, Send, Channel)
   326→    ├── handler.rs     # serenity EventHandler (inbound + OTP + interactions)
   327→    ├── outbound.rs    # ChannelOutbound + ChannelStreamOutbound impls
   328→    ├── plugin.rs      # ChannelPlugin + ChannelStatus impls
   329→    └── state.rs       # AccountState + AccountStateMap (includes OtpState)
   330→```
   331→
   332→The crate implements the same trait set as `moltis-telegram` and `moltis-msteams`:
   333→
   334→| Trait | Purpose |
   335→|-------|---------|
   336→| `ChannelPlugin` | Start/stop accounts, lifecycle management |
   337→| `ChannelOutbound` | Send text, media, typing indicators |
   338→| `ChannelStreamOutbound` | Handle streaming responses |
   339→| `ChannelStatus` | Health probes (connected / disconnected) |
   340→
   341→## Troubleshooting
   342→
   343→### Bot doesn't respond
   344→
   345→- Verify **Message Content Intent** is enabled in the Developer Portal
   346→- Check that the bot token is correct (reset it if unsure)
   347→- Ensure the bot has been invited to the server with the right permissions
   348→- Check `dm_policy` / `group_policy` — if set to `"allowlist"`, make sure
   349→  your username or guild ID is listed
   350→- Look at logs: `RUST_LOG=moltis_discord=debug moltis`
   351→
   352→### "Gateway connection failed"
   353→
   354→- Check your network connection — the bot connects outward to
   355→  `wss://gateway.discord.gg`
   356→- Firewalls or proxies that block outbound WebSocket connections will prevent
   357→  the bot from connecting
   358→- The token may have been revoked — regenerate it in the Developer Portal
   359→
   360→### Bot responds in DMs but not in guilds
   361→
   362→- Check `mention_mode` — if set to `"mention"`, you must @mention the bot
   363→- Check `group_policy` — if `"disabled"`, guild messages are ignored
   364→- Check `guild_allowlist` — if non-empty, the guild must be listed

file.content:
# Discord

Moltis can connect to Discord as a bot, letting you chat with your agent from
any Discord server or DM. The integration uses Discord's
[Gateway API](https://discord.com/developers/docs/events/gateway) via a
persistent WebSocket connection — no public URL or webhook endpoint is required.

## How It Works

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   Discord Gateway                     │
│              (wss://gateway.discord.gg)               │
└──────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
                   │  persistent WebSocket
                   ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│               moltis-discord crate                    │
│  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  │
│  │  Handler   │  │  Outbound  │  │     Plugin     │  │
│  │ (inbound)  │  │ (replies)  │  │  (lifecycle)   │  │
│  └────────────┘  └────────────┘  └────────────────┘  │
└──────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
                   │
                   ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 Moltis Gateway                        │
│         (chat dispatch, tools, memory)                │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The bot connects **outward** to Discord's servers. Unlike Microsoft Teams
(which requires an inbound webhook), Discord needs no port forwarding, no
public domain, and no TLS certificate. This makes it especially easy to run
on a home machine or behind a NAT.

## Prerequisites

Before configuring Moltis, create a Discord bot:

1. Go to the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications)
2. Click **New Application** and give it a name
3. Navigate to **Bot** in the left sidebar
4. Click **Reset Token** and copy the bot token
5. Under **Privileged Gateway Intents**, enable **Message Content Intent**
6. Navigate to **OAuth2 → URL Generator**
   - Scopes: `bot`
   - Bot Permissions: `Send Messages`, `Attach Files`, `Read Message History`, `Add Reactions`
7. Copy the generated URL and open it to invite the bot to your server

```admonish warning
The bot token is a secret — treat it like a password. Never commit it to
version control. Moltis stores it with `secrecy::Secret` and redacts it from
logs, but your `moltis.toml` file is plain text on disk. Consider using
[Vault](vault.md) for encryption at rest.
```

## Configuration

Add a `[channels.discord.<account-id>]` section to your `moltis.toml`:

```toml
[channels.discord.my-bot]
token = "MTIzNDU2Nzg5.example.bot-token"
```

Make sure `"discord"` is included in `channels.offered` so the Web UI shows
the Discord option:

```toml
[channels]
offered = ["telegram", "discord"]
```

### Configuration Fields

| Field | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `token` | **yes** | — | Discord bot token from the Developer Portal |
| `dm_policy` | no | `"allowlist"` | Who can DM the bot: `"open"`, `"allowlist"`, or `"disabled"` |
| `group_policy` | no | `"open"` | Who can talk to the bot in guild channels: `"open"`, `"allowlist"`, or `"disabled"` |
| `mention_mode` | no | `"mention"` | When the bot responds in guilds: `"always"`, `"mention"` (only when @mentioned), or `"none"` |
| `allowlist` | no | `[]` | Discord usernames allowed to DM the bot (when `dm_policy = "allowlist"`) |
| `guild_allowlist` | no | `[]` | Guild (server) IDs allowed to interact with the bot |
| `model` | no | — | Override the default model for this channel |
| `model_provider` | no | — | Provider for the overridden model |
| `reply_to_message` | no | `false` | Send bot responses as Discord replies to the user's message |
| `ack_reaction` | no | — | Emoji reaction added while processing (e.g. `"👀"`); omit to disable |
| `activity` | no | — | Bot activity status text (e.g. `"with AI"`) |
| `activity_type` | no | `"custom"` | Activity type: `"playing"`, `"listening"`, `"watching"`, `"competing"`, or `"custom"` |
| `status` | no | `"online"` | Bot online status: `"online"`, `"idle"`, `"dnd"`, or `"invisible"` |
| `otp_self_approval` | no | `true` | Enable OTP self-approval for non-allowlisted DM users |
| `otp_cooldown_secs` | no | `300` | Cooldown in seconds after 3 failed OTP attempts |

### Full Example

```toml
[channels]
offered = ["telegram", "discord"]

[channels.discord.my-bot]
token = "MTIzNDU2Nzg5.example.bot-token"
dm_policy = "allowlist"
group_policy = "open"
mention_mode = "mention"
allowlist = ["alice", "bob"]
guild_allowlist = ["123456789012345678"]
reply_to_message = true
ack_reaction = "👀"
model = "gpt-4o"
model_provider = "openai"
activity = "with AI"
activity_type = "custom"
status = "online"
otp_self_approval = true
```

## Access Control

Discord uses the same gating system as Telegram and Microsoft Teams:

### DM Policy

Controls who can send direct messages to the bot.

| Value | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| `"allowlist"` | Only users listed in `allowlist` can DM (default) |
| `"open"` | Anyone who can reach the bot can DM it |
| `"disabled"` | DMs are silently ignored |

### Group Policy

Controls who can interact with the bot in guild (server) channels.

| Value | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| `"open"` | Bot responds in any guild channel (default) |
| `"allowlist"` | Only guilds listed in `guild_allowlist` are allowed |
| `"disabled"` | Guild messages are silently ignored |

### Mention Mode

Controls when the bot responds in guild channels (does not apply to DMs).

| Value | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| `"mention"` | Bot only responds when @mentioned (default) |
| `"always"` | Bot responds to every message in allowed channels |
| `"none"` | Bot never responds in guilds (useful for DM-only bots) |

### Guild Allowlist

If `guild_allowlist` is non-empty, messages from guilds **not** in the list are
silently dropped — regardless of `group_policy`. This provides a server-level
filter on top of the channel-level policy.

### OTP Self-Approval

When `dm_policy = "allowlist"` and `otp_self_approval = true` (the default),
unknown users who DM the bot receive a verification challenge. The flow:

1. User sends a DM to the bot
2. Bot responds with a challenge prompt (the 6-digit code is **not** shown to the user)
3. The code appears in the Moltis web UI under **Channels → Senders**
4. The bot owner shares the code with the user out-of-band
5. User replies with the 6-digit code
6. On success, the user is automatically added to the allowlist

After 3 failed attempts, the user is locked out for `otp_cooldown_secs` seconds
(default: 300). Codes expire after 5 minutes.

```admonish tip
This is the same OTP mechanism used by the Telegram integration. It provides a
simple access control flow without requiring manual allowlist management.
```

## Bot Presence

Configure the bot's Discord presence (the "Playing..." / "Listening to..." status)
using the `activity`, `activity_type`, and `status` fields:

```toml
[channels.discord.my-bot]
token = "..."
activity = "with AI"
activity_type = "custom"  # or "playing", "listening", "watching", "competing"
status = "online"         # or "idle", "dnd", "invisible"
```

The presence is set when the bot connects to the Discord gateway. If no activity
or status is configured, the bot uses Discord's default (online, no activity).

## Slash Commands

The bot automatically registers native Discord slash commands when it connects:

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/new` | Start a new chat session |
| `/clear` | Clear the current session history |
| `/compact` | Summarize the current session |
| `/context` | Show session info (model, tokens, plugins) |
| `/model` | List or switch the AI model |
| `/sessions` | List or switch chat sessions |
| `/agent` | List or switch agents |
| `/help` | Show available commands |

Slash commands appear in Discord's command palette (type `/` in any channel where
the bot is present). Responses are ephemeral — only visible to the user who
invoked the command.

```admonish note
Text-based `/` commands (e.g. typing `/model` as a regular message) continue to
work alongside native slash commands. The native commands provide autocomplete and
a better Discord-native experience.
```

## Web UI Setup

You can also configure Discord through the web interface:

1. Open **Settings → Channels**
2. Click **Connect Discord**
3. Enter an account ID (any alias) and your bot token
4. Adjust DM policy, mention mode, and allowlist as needed
5. Click **Connect**

The same form is available during onboarding when Discord is in `channels.offered`.

## Talking to Your Bot

Once the bot is connected there are several ways to interact with it.

### In a Server

To use the bot in a Discord server you need to invite it first:

1. Go to the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications)
2. Select your application → **OAuth2 → URL Generator**
3. Scopes: check **bot**
4. Bot Permissions: check **Send Messages**, **Read Message History**, and **Add Reactions**
5. Copy the generated URL and open it in your browser
6. Select the server you want to add the bot to and confirm

```admonish tip
The Moltis web UI generates this invite link automatically when you paste your
bot token. Look for the "Invite bot to a server" card in the Connect Discord
dialog.
```

Once the bot is in your server, **@mention** it in any channel to get a
response (assuming `mention_mode = "mention"`, the default). If you set
`mention_mode = "always"` the bot responds to every message in allowed channels.

### Via Direct Message

You can DM the bot directly from Discord — no shared server required:

1. Open Discord and go to **Direct Messages**
2. Click the **New Message** icon (or **Find or start a conversation**)
3. Search for the bot's username and select it
4. Send a message

```admonish note
If `dm_policy` is set to `"allowlist"` (the default), make sure your Discord
username is listed in the `allowlist` array — otherwise the bot will ignore your
DMs. Set `dm_policy = "open"` to allow anyone to DM the bot.
```

### Without a Shared Server

DMs work even if you and the bot don't share a server. Discord bots are
reachable by username from any account. This makes DMs the simplest way to
start chatting — just connect the bot in Moltis and message it directly.

## Message Handling

### Inbound Messages

When a message arrives from Discord:

1. Bot's own messages are ignored
2. Guild allowlist is checked (if configured)
3. DM/group policy is evaluated
4. Mention mode is checked (guild messages only)
5. Bot mention prefix (`@BotName`) is stripped from the message text
6. The message is logged and dispatched to the chat engine
7. Commands (messages starting with `/`) are dispatched to the command handler

### Outbound Messages

Discord enforces a **2,000-character limit** per message. Moltis automatically
splits long responses into multiple messages, preferring to break at newline
boundaries and avoiding splits inside fenced code blocks.

Streaming uses **edit-in-place** — an initial message is sent after 30 characters
and then updated every 500ms as tokens arrive. If the final text exceeds 2,000
characters, the first message is edited to the limit and overflow is sent as
follow-up messages.

### Reply-to-Message

Set `reply_to_message = true` to have the bot send responses as Discord replies
(threaded to the user's original message). The first chunk of a multi-chunk or
streamed response carries the reply reference; follow-up chunks are sent as
regular messages.

### Ack Reactions

Set `ack_reaction = "👀"` (or any Unicode emoji) to have the bot react to the
user's message when processing starts. The reaction is removed once the response
is complete. This provides a visual indicator that the bot has seen the message
and is working on a reply.

## Crate Structure

```
crates/discord/
├── Cargo.toml
└── src/
    ├── lib.rs         # Public exports
    ├── commands.rs    # Native Discord slash command registration
    ├── config.rs      # DiscordAccountConfig (token, policies, presence, OTP)
    ├── error.rs       # Error enum (Config, Gateway, Send, Channel)
    ├── handler.rs     # serenity EventHandler (inbound + OTP + interactions)
    ├── outbound.rs    # ChannelOutbound + ChannelStreamOutbound impls
    ├── plugin.rs      # ChannelPlugin + ChannelStatus impls
    └── state.rs       # AccountState + AccountStateMap (includes OtpState)
```

The crate implements the same trait set as `moltis-telegram` and `moltis-msteams`:

| Trait | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `ChannelPlugin` | Start/stop accounts, lifecycle management |
| `ChannelOutbound` | Send text, media, typing indicators |
| `ChannelStreamOutbound` | Handle streaming responses |
| `ChannelStatus` | Health probes (connected / disconnected) |

## Troubleshooting

### Bot doesn't respond

- Verify **Message Content Intent** is enabled in the Developer Portal
- Check that the bot token is correct (reset it if unsure)
- Ensure the bot has been invited to the server with the right permissions
- Check `dm_policy` / `group_policy` — if set to `"allowlist"`, make sure
  your username or guild ID is listed
- Look at logs: `RUST_LOG=moltis_discord=debug moltis`

### "Gateway connection failed"

- Check your network connection — the bot connects outward to
  `wss://gateway.discord.gg`
- Firewalls or proxies that block outbound WebSocket connections will prevent
  the bot from connecting
- The token may have been revoked — regenerate it in the Developer Portal

### Bot responds in DMs but not in guilds

- Check `mention_mode` — if set to `"mention"`, you must @mention the bot
- Check `group_policy` — if `"disabled"`, guild messages are ignored
- Check `guild_allowlist` — if non-empty, the guild must be listed

1→//! Default configuration template with all options documented.
     2→//!
     3→//! This template is used when creating a new config file. It includes all
     4→//! available options with descriptions, allowing users to see everything
     5→//! that can be configured even if they don't change the defaults.
     6→
     7→/// Generate the default config template with a specific port.
     8→pub fn default_config_template(port: u16) -> String {
     9→    format!(
    10→        r##"# Moltis Configuration
    11→# ====================
    12→# This file contains all available configuration options.
    13→# Uncomment and modify settings as needed.
    14→# Changes require a restart to take effect.
    15→#
    16→# Environment variable substitution is supported: ${{ENV_VAR}}
    17→# Example: api_key = "${{ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}}"
    18→
    19→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    20→# SERVER
    21→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    22→
    23→[server]
    24→bind = "127.0.0.1"                # Address to bind to ("0.0.0.0" for all interfaces)
    25→port = {port}                           # Port number (auto-generated for this installation)
    26→http_request_logs = false              # Enable verbose Axum HTTP request/response logs (debugging)
    27→ws_request_logs = false                # Enable WebSocket RPC request/response logs (debugging)
    28→update_releases_url = "https://www.moltis.org/releases.json"    # Releases manifest URL for update checks (override to use a custom URL)
    29→
    30→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    31→# AUTHENTICATION
    32→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    33→
    34→[auth]
    35→disabled = false                  # true = disable auth entirely (DANGEROUS if exposed)
    36→                                  # When disabled, anyone with network access can use moltis
    37→
    38→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    39→# GRAPHQL
    40→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    41→
    42→[graphql]
    43→enabled = false                   # Enable GraphQL endpoint (/graphql for HTTP + WebSocket)
    44→                                  # Can be toggled at runtime in Settings > GraphQL
    45→
    46→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    47→# TLS / HTTPS
    48→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    49→
    50→[tls]
    51→enabled = true                    # Enable HTTPS (recommended)
    52→auto_generate = true              # Auto-generate local CA and server certificate
    53→# http_redirect_port = 18790      # Optional override (default: server.port + 1)
    54→# cert_path = "/path/to/cert.pem"     # Custom certificate file (overrides auto-gen)
    55→# key_path = "/path/to/key.pem"       # Custom private key file
    56→# ca_cert_path = "/path/to/ca.pem"    # CA certificate for trust instructions
    57→
    58→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    59→# AGENT IDENTITY
    60→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    61→# Customize your agent's personality. These are typically set during onboarding.
    62→
    63→[identity]
    64→# name = "moltis"                 # Agent's display name
    65→# emoji = "🦊"                    # Agent's emoji/avatar
    66→# theme = "wise owl"              # Theme for agent personality (e.g. wise owl, chill fox)
    67→# soul = ""                       # Freeform personality text injected into system prompt
    68→                                  # Use this for custom instructions, tone, or behavior
    69→
    70→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    71→# USER PROFILE
    72→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    73→# Information about you. Set during onboarding.
    74→
    75→[user]
    76→# name = "Your Name"              # Your name (used in conversations)
    77→# timezone = "America/New_York"   # Your timezone (IANA format)
    78→
    79→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    80→# LLM PROVIDERS
    81→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    82→# Configure API keys and settings for each LLM provider.
    83→# API keys can also be set via environment variables (preferred for security).
    84→#
    85→# Each provider supports:
    86→#   enabled   - Whether to use this provider (default: true)
    87→#   api_key   - API key (or use env var like ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
    88→#   base_url  - Override API endpoint
    89→#   models    - Preferred models shown first (optional)
    90→#   fetch_models - Discover models from provider API when available (default: true)
    91→#   stream_transport - Streaming transport: "sse", "websocket", or "auto" (default: "sse")
    92→#   alias     - Custom name for metrics labels (useful for multiple instances)
    93→
    94→[providers]
    95→offered = ["local-llm", "github-copilot", "openai-codex", "openai", "anthropic", "openrouter", "ollama", "moonshot", "minimax", "zai"] # Enabled providers and those shown in onboarding/picker UI ([] = enable/show all)
    96→# All available providers:
    97→#   "anthropic", "openai", "gemini", "groq", "xai", "deepseek",
    98→#   "mistral", "openrouter", "cerebras", "minimax", "moonshot",
    99→#   "zai", "venice", "ollama", "local-llm", "openai-codex",
   100→#   "github-copilot", "kimi-code"
   101→
   102→# ── Anthropic (Claude) ────────────────────────────────────────
   103→# [providers.anthropic]
   104→# enabled = true
   105→# api_key = "sk-ant-..."                      # Or set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var
   106→# models = ["claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"]     # Optional preferred models
   107→# fetch_models = true                          # Set false to skip remote discovery
   108→# base_url = "https://api.anthropic.com"     # API endpoint
   109→# alias = "anthropic"                         # Custom name for metrics
   110→
   111→# ── OpenAI ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   112→[providers.openai]
   113→# enabled = true
   114→# api_key = "sk-..."                          # Or set OPENAI_API_KEY env var
   115→models = ["gpt-5.3", "gpt-5.2"]              # Preferred models shown first
   116→# fetch_models = true
   117→# stream_transport = "sse"                     # "sse" | "websocket" | "auto"
   118→# base_url = "https://api.openai.com/v1"     # API endpoint (change for Azure, etc.)
   119→# alias = "openai"
   120→
   121→# ── Google Gemini ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   122→# [providers.gemini]
   123→# enabled = true
   124→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set GEMINI_API_KEY / GOOGLE_API_KEY env var
   125→# models = ["gemini-2.5-flash-preview-05-20", "gemini-2.0-flash"]
   126→# fetch_models = true
   127→# base_url = "https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai"
   128→# alias = "gemini"
   129→
   130→# ── Groq ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   131→# [providers.groq]
   132→# enabled = true
   133→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set GROQ_API_KEY env var
   134→# models = ["llama-3.3-70b-versatile"]
   135→# alias = "groq"
   136→
   137→# ── DeepSeek ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
   138→# [providers.deepseek]
   139→# enabled = true
   140→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set DEEPSEEK_API_KEY env var
   141→# models = ["deepseek-chat"]
   142→# base_url = "https://api.deepseek.com"
   143→# alias = "deepseek"
   144→
   145→# ── xAI (Grok) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   146→# [providers.xai]
   147→# enabled = true
   148→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set XAI_API_KEY env var
   149→# models = ["grok-3-mini"]
   150→# alias = "xai"
   151→
   152→# ── OpenRouter (multi-provider gateway) ───────────────────────
   153→# [providers.openrouter]
   154→# enabled = true
   155→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var
   156→# models = ["anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet"]    # Any model IDs on OpenRouter
   157→# base_url = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
   158→
   159→# ── Moonshot (Kimi) ─────────────────────────────────────────
   160→[providers.moonshot]
   161→# enabled = true
   162→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set MOONSHOT_API_KEY env var
   163→models = ["kimi-k2.5"]                        # Preferred models shown first
   164→# base_url = "https://api.moonshot.ai/v1"
   165→# alias = "moonshot"
   166→
   167→[providers.ollama]
   168→# base_url = "http://localhost:11434"
   169→# models = ["llama3.2", "qwen2.5:7b"]         # Optional preferred models; installed models are discovered dynamically
   170→
   171→[providers.local-llm]
   172→# models = ["qwen2.5-coder-7b-q4_k_m"]        # Optional; configure local models in onboarding
   173→
   174→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   175→# CHAT SETTINGS
   176→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   177→
   178→[chat]
   179→message_queue_mode = "followup"   # Default: process queued messages one-by-one after the current run.
   180→                                  # How to handle messages during an active agent run:
   181→                                  #   "followup" - Queue messages, replay one-by-one after run
   182→                                  #   "collect"  - Buffer messages, concatenate as single message
   183→# priority_models = ["claude-opus-4-5", "gpt-5.2", "gemini-3-flash"]  # Optional: models to pin first in selectors
   184→# allowed_models = ["gpt 5.2"]  # Legacy field (currently ignored).
   185→
   186→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   187→# SPAWN PRESETS (OPTIONAL)
   188→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   189→# Configure reusable presets for the `spawn_agent` tool.
   190→#
   191→# [agents]
   192→# default_preset = "research"      # Optional: used when spawn_agent.preset is omitted
   193→#
   194→# [agents.presets.research]
   195→# model = "openai/gpt-5.2"
   196→# allow_tools = ["web_search", "web_fetch", "sessions_send", "task_list"]
   197→# deny_tools = ["exec"]
   198→# delegate_only = false
   199→# system_prompt_suffix = "Focus on gathering and summarizing evidence."
   200→
   201→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   202→# TOOLS
   203→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   204→
   205→[tools]
   206→agent_timeout_secs = 600          # Max seconds for an agent run (0 = no timeout)
   207→agent_max_iterations = 25         # Max LLM/tool loop iterations before stopping
   208→max_tool_result_bytes = 50000     # Max bytes per tool result before truncation (50KB)
   209→
   210→# ── Maps ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   211→
   212→[tools.maps]
   213→provider = "google_maps"          # Map provider used by show_map:
   214→                                  #   "google_maps" (default)
   215→                                  #   "apple_maps"
   216→                                  #   "openstreetmap"
   217→
   218→# ── Command Execution ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   219→
   220→[tools.exec]
   221→default_timeout_secs = 30         # Default timeout for commands
   222→max_output_bytes = 204800         # Max command output bytes (200KB)
   223→approval_mode = "on-miss"         # When to require approval:
   224→                                  #   "always"  - Always ask before running
   225→                                  #   "on-miss" - Ask if not in allowlist
   226→                                  #   "never"   - Never ask (dangerous)
   227→security_level = "allowlist"      # Security mode:
   228→                                  #   "permissive" - Allow most commands
   229→                                  #   "allowlist"  - Only allow listed commands
   230→                                  #   "strict"     - Very restrictive
   231→allowlist = []                    # Command patterns to allow (when security_level = "allowlist")
   232→                                  # Example: ["git *", "npm *", "cargo *"]
   233→
   234→# ── Sandbox Configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   235→# Commands run inside isolated containers for security.
   236→
   237→[tools.exec.sandbox]
   238→mode = "all"                      # Which commands to sandbox:
   239→                                  #   "off"      - No sandboxing (commands run on host)
   240→                                  #   "non-main" - Sandbox all except main session
   241→                                  #   "all"      - Sandbox everything (recommended)
   242→scope = "session"                 # Container lifecycle:
   243→                                  #   "command" - New container per command
   244→                                  #   "session" - Container per session (recommended)
   245→                                  #   "global"  - Single shared container
   246→workspace_mount = "ro"            # How to mount workspace in sandbox:
   247→                                  #   "ro"   - Read-only (safe)
   248→                                  #   "rw"   - Read-write (can modify files)
   249→                                  #   "none" - No mount
   250→home_persistence = "shared"       # Persist /home/sandbox across container recreation:
   251→                                  #   "off"     - Ephemeral home
   252→                                  #   "session" - Per-session persisted home
   253→                                  #   "shared"  - One shared persisted home (default)
   254→# shared_home_dir = "/path/to/shared-home"  # Host dir for shared persistence (default: data_dir()/sandbox/home/shared)
   255→backend = "auto"                  # Container backend:
   256→                                  #   "auto"            - Auto-detect (prefers Apple Container on macOS)
   257→                                  #   "docker"          - Use Docker
   258→                                  #   "apple-container" - Use Apple Container (macOS only)
   259→no_network = true                 # Disable network access in sandbox (recommended)
   260→# image = "custom-image:tag"      # Custom Docker image (default: auto-built)
   261→# container_prefix = "moltis"     # Prefix for container names
   262→
   263→# Packages installed in sandbox containers via apt-get.
   264→# This list is used to build the sandbox image. Customize as needed.
   265→packages = [
   266→    # Networking & HTTP
   267→    "curl",
   268→    "wget",
   269→    "ca-certificates",
   270→    "dnsutils",
   271→    "netcat-openbsd",
   272→    "openssh-client",
   273→    "iproute2",
   274→    "net-tools",
   275→    # Language runtimes
   276→    "python3",
   277→    "python3-dev",
   278→    "python3-pip",
   279→    "python3-venv",
   280→    "python-is-python3",
   281→    "nodejs",
   282→    "npm",
   283→    "ruby",
   284→    "ruby-dev",
   285→    "golang-go",
   286→    # Build toolchain & native deps
   287→    "build-essential",
   288→    "clang",
   289→    "libclang-dev",
   290→    "llvm-dev",
   291→    "pkg-config",
   292→    "libssl-dev",
   293→    "libsqlite3-dev",
   294→    "libyaml-dev",
   295→    "liblzma-dev",
   296→    "autoconf",
   297→    "automake",
   298→    "libtool",
   299→    "bison",
   300→    "flex",
   301→    "dpkg-dev",
   302→    "fakeroot",
   303→    # Compression & archiving
   304→    "zip",
   305→    "unzip",
   306→    "bzip2",
   307→    "xz-utils",
   308→    "p7zip-full",
   309→    "tar",
   310→    "zstd",
   311→    "lz4",
   312→    "pigz",
   313→    # Common CLI utilities
   314→    "git",
   315→    "gnupg2",
   316→    "jq",
   317→    "rsync",
   318→    "file",
   319→    "tree",
   320→    "sqlite3",
   321→    "sudo",
   322→    "locales",
   323→    "tzdata",
   324→    "shellcheck",
   325→    "patchelf",
   326→    "tmux",
   327→    # Text processing & search
   328→    "ripgrep",
   329→    # Browser automation dependencies
   330→    "chromium",
   331→    "libxss1",
   332→    "libnss3",
   333→    "libnspr4",
   334→    "libasound2t64",
   335→    "libatk1.0-0t64",
   336→    "libatk-bridge2.0-0t64",
   337→    "libcups2t64",
   338→    "libdrm2",
   339→    "libgbm1",
   340→    "libgtk-3-0t64",
   341→    "libxcomposite1",
   342→    "libxdamage1",
   343→    "libxfixes3",
   344→    "libxrandr2",
   345→    "libxkbcommon0",
   346→    "fonts-liberation",
   347→]
   348→
   349→# Resource limits for sandboxed execution (optional)
   350→[tools.exec.sandbox.resource_limits]
   351→# memory_limit = "512M"           # Memory limit (e.g., "512M", "1G", "2G")
   352→# cpu_quota = 0.5                 # CPU quota as fraction (0.5 = half a core, 2.0 = two cores)
   353→# pids_max = 100                  # Maximum number of processes
   354→
   355→# ── Tool Policy ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   356→# Control which tools agents can use.
   357→
   358→[tools.policy]
   359→allow = []                        # Tools to always allow (e.g., ["exec", "web_fetch"])
   360→deny = []                         # Tools to always deny (e.g., ["browser"])
   361→# profile = "default"             # Named policy profile
   362→
   363→# ── Web Search ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   364→
   365→[tools.web.search]
   366→enabled = true                    # Enable web search tool
   367→provider = "brave"                # Search provider: "brave" or "perplexity"
   368→max_results = 5                   # Number of results to return (1-10)
   369→timeout_seconds = 30              # HTTP request timeout
   370→cache_ttl_minutes = 15            # Cache results for this many minutes (0 = no cache)
   371→duckduckgo_fallback = false       # Off by default; enable only if you want DDG fallback without API keys
   372→# api_key = "..."                 # Brave API key (or set BRAVE_API_KEY env var)
   373→
   374→# Perplexity-specific settings (when provider = "perplexity")
   375→[tools.web.search.perplexity]
   376→# api_key = "..."                 # Or set PERPLEXITY_API_KEY env var
   377→# base_url = "..."                # API base URL (auto-detected from key prefix)
   378→# model = "sonar"                 # Perplexity model to use
   379→
   380→# ── Web Fetch ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   381→
   382→[tools.web.fetch]
   383→enabled = true                    # Enable web fetch tool
   384→max_chars = 50000                 # Max characters to return from fetched content
   385→timeout_seconds = 30              # HTTP request timeout
   386→cache_ttl_minutes = 15            # Cache fetched pages for this many minutes (0 = no cache)
   387→max_redirects = 3                 # Maximum HTTP redirects to follow
   388→readability = true                # Use readability extraction for HTML (cleaner output)
   389→# ssrf_allowlist = ["172.22.0.0/16"] # CIDR ranges exempt from SSRF blocking (e.g. Docker networks)
   390→
   391→# ── Browser Automation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   392→# Full browser control via Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP).
   393→# Use for JavaScript-heavy sites, form filling, screenshots.
   394→
   395→[tools.browser]
   396→enabled = true                    # Enable browser tool
   397→headless = true                   # Run without visible window (true = background)
   398→viewport_width = 2560             # Default viewport width in pixels (QHD for tech users)
   399→viewport_height = 1440            # Default viewport height in pixels
   400→device_scale_factor = 2.0         # HiDPI/Retina scaling (2.0 = Retina, 1.0 = standard)
   401→max_instances = 3                 # Maximum concurrent browser instances
   402→idle_timeout_secs = 300           # Close idle browsers after this many seconds (5 min)
   403→navigation_timeout_ms = 30000     # Page load timeout in milliseconds (30 sec)
   404→sandbox = false                   # Run browser in Docker/Apple Container for isolation
   405→# container_host = "127.0.0.1"   # Host/IP to reach browser container (default: localhost)
   406→                                  # Set to "host.docker.internal" when Moltis runs inside Docker
   407→# chrome_path = "/path/to/chrome" # Custom Chrome/Chromium binary path (auto-detected)
   408→# user_agent = "Custom UA"        # Custom user agent string
   409→# chrome_args = []                # Extra Chrome command-line arguments
   410→                                  # Example: ["--disable-extensions", "--disable-gpu"]
   411→
   412→# Domain restrictions for security.
   413→# When set, browser will refuse to navigate to domains not in this list.
   414→# This helps prevent prompt injection from untrusted websites.
   415→allowed_domains = []              # Empty = all domains allowed
   416→# allowed_domains = [
   417→#     "docs.example.com",         # Exact match
   418→#     "*.github.com",             # Wildcard: matches any subdomain of github.com
   419→#     "localhost",                # Allow localhost
   420→#     "127.0.0.1",
   421→# ]
   422→
   423→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   424→# SKILLS
   425→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   426→# Reusable prompt templates and workflows.
   427→
   428→[skills]
   429→enabled = true                    # Enable skills system
   430→search_paths = []                 # Additional directories to search for skills
   431→                                  # Default locations: ~/.config/moltis/skills/, ./skills/
   432→auto_load = []                    # Skills to always load without explicit activation
   433→                                  # Example: ["code-review", "commit"]
   434→
   435→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   436→# MCP SERVERS
   437→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   438→# Model Context Protocol servers provide additional tools and capabilities.
   439→# See https://modelcontextprotocol.io for available servers.
   440→
   441→[mcp]
   442→# Each server has a name and configuration:
   443→#
   444→# [mcp.servers.server-name]
   445→# command = "npx"                 # Command to run (for stdio transport)
   446→# args = ["-y", "@package/name"]  # Command arguments
   447→# env = {{ KEY = "value" }}         # Environment variables for the process
   448→# enabled = true                  # Whether this server is enabled
   449→# transport = "stdio"             # Transport: "stdio" (default) or "sse"
   450→# url = "http://..."              # URL for SSE transport
   451→
   452→# Example: Filesystem access
   453→# [mcp.servers.filesystem]
   454→# command = "npx"
   455→# args = ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/path/to/allow"]
   456→# enabled = true
   457→
   458→# Example: GitHub integration
   459→# [mcp.servers.github]
   460→# command = "npx"
   461→# args = ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
   462→# env = {{ GITHUB_TOKEN = "${{GITHUB_TOKEN}}" }}
   463→# enabled = true
   464→
   465→# Example: SSE server
   466→# [mcp.servers.remote]
   467→# transport = "sse"
   468→# url = "http://localhost:8080/mcp"
   469→# enabled = true
   470→
   471→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   472→# METRICS
   473→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   474→# Prometheus metrics for observability.
   475→
   476→[metrics]
   477→enabled = true                    # Enable metrics collection
   478→prometheus_endpoint = true        # Expose /metrics endpoint for Prometheus scraping
   479→# labels = {{ environment = "production", instance = "main" }}
   480→                                  # Additional labels to add to all metrics
   481→
   482→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   483→# HEARTBEAT
   484→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   485→# Periodic health-check agent turns to keep the agent "alive" and responsive.
   486→
   487→[heartbeat]
   488→enabled = true                    # Enable periodic heartbeats
   489→every = "30m"                     # Interval between heartbeats (e.g., "30m", "1h", "6h")
   490→# model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"  # Override model for heartbeats
   491→# prompt = "..."                  # Custom heartbeat prompt (default: built-in)
   492→ack_max_chars = 300               # Max characters for acknowledgment reply
   493→deliver = false                   # Deliver heartbeat replies to a channel account
   494→# channel = "my-bot"              # Channel account identifier (required when deliver = true)
   495→# to = "123456789"                # Chat/recipient ID (required when deliver = true)
   496→sandbox_enabled = true            # Run heartbeat commands in sandbox
   497→# sandbox_image = "..."           # Override sandbox image for heartbeats
   498→
   499→# Active hours window - heartbeats only run during this time
   500→[heartbeat.active_hours]
   501→start = "08:00"                   # Start time (HH:MM, 24-hour format)
   502→end = "24:00"                     # End time (HH:MM, "24:00" = end of day)
   503→timezone = "local"                # Timezone: "local" or IANA name like "Europe/Paris"
   504→
   505→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   506→# FAILOVER
   507→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   508→# Automatic fallback to alternative models/providers on failure.
   509→
   510→[failover]
   511→enabled = true                    # Enable automatic failover
   512→fallback_models = []              # Ordered list of fallback models
   513→                                  # Empty = auto-build chain from all registered models
   514→                                  # Example: ["openai/gpt-4o", "anthropic/claude-3-haiku"]
   515→
   516→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   517→# VOICE
   518→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   519→# Voice provider settings for text-to-speech (TTS) and speech-to-text (STT).
   520→# `providers` controls what appears in the Settings UI provider list.
   521→
   522→[voice.tts]
   523→enabled = true                    # Enable text-to-speech
   524→# provider = "openai"             # Active TTS provider (auto-selects first configured if omitted)
   525→providers = ["openai", "elevenlabs"] # UI allowlist (empty = show all TTS providers)
   526→# All available TTS providers:
   527→#   "openai", "elevenlabs", "google", "piper", "coqui"
   528→
   529→[voice.stt]
   530→enabled = true                    # Enable speech-to-text
   531→# provider = "whisper"            # Active STT provider (auto-selects first configured if omitted)
   532→providers = ["whisper", "mistral", "elevenlabs"] # UI allowlist (empty = show all STT providers)
   533→# All available STT providers:
   534→#   "whisper", "groq", "deepgram", "google", "mistral",
   535→#   "voxtral-local", "whisper-cli", "sherpa-onnx", "elevenlabs-stt"
   536→
   537→# No api_key needed for OpenAI TTS/Whisper when OpenAI is configured as an LLM provider.
   538→# [voice.tts.openai]
   539→# voice = "alloy"                 # alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, shimmer
   540→# model = "tts-1"                 # tts-1 or tts-1-hd
   541→
   542→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   543→# TAILSCALE
   544→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   545→# Expose moltis via Tailscale Serve (private) or Funnel (public).
   546→
   547→[tailscale]
   548→mode = "off"                      # Tailscale mode:
   549→                                  #   "off"    - Disabled
   550→                                  #   "serve"  - Tailnet-only HTTPS (private)
   551→                                  #   "funnel" - Public HTTPS via Tailscale
   552→reset_on_exit = true              # Reset serve/funnel when gateway shuts down
   553→
   554→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   555→# MEMORY / EMBEDDINGS
   556→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   557→# Configure the embedding provider for memory/RAG features.
   558→
   559→[memory]
   560→# provider = "local"              # Embedding provider:
   561→                                  #   "local"   - Built-in local embeddings
   562→                                  #   "ollama"  - Ollama server
   563→                                  #   "openai"  - OpenAI API
   564→                                  #   "custom"  - Custom endpoint
   565→                                  #   (none)    - Auto-detect from available providers
   566→# disable_rag = false             # true => keyword-only search (no embeddings)
   567→# base_url = "http://localhost:11434/v1"  # Embedding API base (host, /v1, or /embeddings)
   568→# model = "nomic-embed-text"      # Embedding model name
   569→# api_key = "..."                 # API key (optional for local endpoints like Ollama)
   570→
   571→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   572→# CHANNELS
   573→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   574→# External messaging integrations.
   575→
   576→[channels]
   577→# Telegram bots
   578→# [channels.telegram.my-bot]
   579→# token = "..."                   # Bot token from @BotFather
   580→# allowed_users = []              # Telegram user IDs allowed to chat (empty = all)
   581→
   582→# Microsoft Teams bots
   583→# [channels.msteams.my-bot]
   584→# app_id = "..."                  # Azure Bot App ID
   585→# app_password = "..."            # Azure Bot App Password (client secret)
   586→# webhook_secret = "..."          # Optional query secret for webhook URL (?secret=...)
   587→# allowlist = []                  # User IDs allowed to DM (empty = all unless dm_policy=allowlist)
   588→
   589→# Discord bots
   590→# [channels.discord.my-bot]
   591→# token = "..."                   # Bot token from Discord Developer Portal
   592→# dm_policy = "allowlist"         # "open", "allowlist", or "disabled"
   593→# group_policy = "open"           # "open", "allowlist", or "disabled"
   594→# mention_mode = "mention"        # "mention", "always", or "none"
   595→# allowlist = []                  # Discord user IDs allowed to DM
   596→# guild_allowlist = []            # Discord guild/server IDs (empty = all)
   597→# reply_to_message = false        # Send responses as Discord replies
   598→# ack_reaction = "👀"             # Emoji reaction while processing (omit to disable)
   599→# activity = "with AI"            # Bot activity status text
   600→# activity_type = "custom"        # "playing", "listening", "watching", "competing", or "custom"
   601→# status = "online"               # "online", "idle", "dnd", or "invisible"
   602→# otp_self_approval = true        # OTP self-approval for non-allowlisted DM users
   603→# otp_cooldown_secs = 300         # Cooldown after 3 failed OTP attempts
   604→
   605→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   606→# HOOKS
   607→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   608→# Shell commands triggered by events.
   609→
   610→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   611→# ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES
   612→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   613→# Variables injected into the Moltis process at startup.
   614→# Useful for API keys in Docker where you can't easily pass env vars.
   615→# Process env vars (docker -e, host env) take precedence — existing vars
   616→# are NOT overwritten.
   617→#
   618→# [env]
   619→# BRAVE_API_KEY = "..."
   620→# OPENROUTER_API_KEY = "sk-or-..."
   621→
   622→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   623→# HOOKS
   624→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   625→# Shell commands triggered by events.
   626→
   627→# [hooks]
   628→# [[hooks.hooks]]
   629→# name = "my-hook"                # Hook name (for logging)
   630→# command = "/path/to/handler.sh" # Command to run
   631→# events = [
   632→#     # ── Modifying events (can block or modify payload) ──
   633→#     "BeforeAgentStart",          # Before the agent loop starts
   634→#     "BeforeLLMCall",             # Before prompt is sent to the LLM provider
   635→#     "AfterLLMCall",              # After LLM response, before tool execution
   636→#     "BeforeToolCall",            # Before a tool executes
   637→#     "BeforeCompaction",          # Before context window compaction
   638→#     "MessageSending",            # Before sending a response to the user
   639→#     "ToolResultPersist",         # When a tool result is persisted
   640→#     #
   641→#     # ── Read-only events (observe only, run in parallel) ──
   642→#     "AfterToolCall",             # After a tool completes
   643→#     "AfterCompaction",           # After context is compacted
   644→#     "AgentEnd",                  # When the agent loop finishes
   645→#     "MessageReceived",           # When a user message arrives
   646→#     "MessageSent",               # After a response is delivered
   647→#     "SessionStart",              # When a new session begins
   648→#     "SessionEnd",                # When a session ends
   649→#     "GatewayStart",              # When Moltis starts
   650→#     "GatewayStop",               # When Moltis shuts down
   651→#     "Command",                   # When a slash command is used
   652→# ]
   653→# timeout = 10                    # Command timeout in seconds
   654→# [hooks.hooks.env]               # Environment variables passed to command
   655→# CUSTOM_VAR = "value"
   656→"##
   657→    )
   658→}
   659→
